key_points: Water exists in solid, liquid, and gaseous states and constantly changes state through the water cycle
Freshwater is limited; only 3% of total water is freshwater, and most is trapped in glaciers
Groundwater is stored in aquifers and is an important source of drinking water
Rainwater harvesting and watershed management help conserve and recharge water
Water pollution occurs due to domestic, industrial, and agricultural wastes
examples: Wells and tube wells access groundwater for drinking and irrigation
Rainwater harvesting in urban areas reduces dependence on rivers and reservoirs
The Ganga and Brahmaputra rivers provide freshwater for millions of people
Industrial effluents contaminate rivers and affect aquatic life
Use of fertilizers and pesticides in agriculture can pollute nearby water sources
formulas: Water cycle → Evaporation + Condensation + Precipitation + Collection
Groundwater recharge = Rainfall infiltrated into soil
exam_tips: Understand the difference between freshwater and saline water
Learn how groundwater is recharged and the importance of aquifers
Focus on causes and effects of water pollution
Include examples of water conservation methods in answers
